batch_metadata.notes: Quiet RF-02 batch. October–November 2023 covers fall school season, Halloween, and Thanksgiving lead-up. No DND enforcement events, no preference relays that close out contact, no visit refusals. Multiple cooperative facilitation instances: Melissa facilitates Olivia-initiated contact (ROW-3728-3729 daycare distress call; ROW-3755 show-and-tell relay; ROW-3793 Olivia wants Eli). Halloween coordination (ROW-3933-3942) was broadly cooperative — Olivia's preference to start at Melissa's then go to Eli's relayed and followed. Visits arranged smoothly (ROW-3814, ROW-3942). This batch serves as an analytical contrast baseline: the relative absence of RF-02 signals here makes the pattern periods in surrounding batches more analytically significant. Single RF-02 signal: ROW-3780 — cutoff framing language ('you treat the cut off time as the target time to call') demonstrating the DND's continued behavioral influence even when not actively enforced. Note: triage estimated 500 messages for this batch; date-based extraction yields 330 actual messages, consistent with anomalous triage row ranges for B-011.

flagged_messages.verbatim_message: Cause you treat the cut off time as the target time to call
flagged_messages.flag_reasoning: Weakest signal type for ARC-RF02-001 — not a blocking event, but a framing statement that reveals the DND cutoff's continued behavioral influence in the co-parenting dynamic. Context: Eli said he didn't call because he thought they were at the fair (ROW-3777); Melissa disputes this and accuses him of calling near the cutoff by habit (ROW-3779: 'You didn't call because you forgot to call per usual'; ROW-3780: 'Cause you treat the cut off time as the target time to call'). Analytically, Melissa's framing assigns Eli behavioral blame via the cutoff structure — the DND cutoff shapes expectations, blame attribution, and Eli's calling patterns even when not actively blocking. This is a subtle but real expression of the gatekeeping architecture: the cutoff functions as a normative scheduling structure that Melissa monitors and polices. Per CA-RF02-01. Sev 1.

unflagged_notable.reason_noted: Melissa facilitates Olivia-initiated contact: 'Olivia's calling cause she's crying not wanting to go to daycare tomorrow. She wants to know if you can pick her up early.' (ROW-3728-3729). Melissa also offers a lunch alternative (ROW-3731-3732). Olivia's emotional distress about daycare prompted both parties to coordinate around Olivia's needs. Strongly cooperative facilitation; noted as counter-pattern evidence.
Melissa proactively relays that 'Olivia wants to tell you about show and tell' — facilitating contact initiated by Olivia's enthusiasm. Cooperative relay. Noted as counter-pattern.
'Olivia wants ya' — Melissa proactively texting Eli to relay that Olivia wants him. Cooperative facilitation of Olivia-initiated contact. Counter-pattern evidence.

Halloween coordination: Melissa invites Eli to trick-or-treat (ROW-3933), Eli proposes his neighborhood (ROW-3934), Melissa relays Olivia's preference to start at Melissa's then go to Eli's (ROW-3935), Eli shows up and participates. The visit happened. Broadly cooperative. ROW-3936 contains Abby-insertion criticism ('You're not trying to get her more candy. You're trying to not fight with Abby.') — flagged as peripheral RF-05 — but the overall Halloween visit was facilitated.

Olivia was in tears when leaving Eli after a school lunch visit (ROW-3966-3967: 'She was in tears again. It's hard leaving and coming back.'). Melissa responds: 'She doesn't cry with me anymore' (ROW-3969). Melissa's comparison functions as a subtle delegitimizer — Olivia cries leaving Eli but not Melissa, framing the Eli-Olivia attachment as distressing rather than strong. Flagged as peripheral RF-04. Noted here for context.

peripheral_flags_summary.note: 'You're not trying to get her more candy. You're trying to not fight with Abby. Olivia is more important.' Context: Eli suggested trick-or-treating in his neighborhood for more candy. Melissa projects Abby-conflict avoidance as Eli's true motive, framing Abby as a controlling factor in his parenting decisions. Consistent with ARC-RF02-005 pattern (systematic Abby insertion as a negative force). RF-05 (derogatory about partner; projecting partner's control over Eli's parenting). Sev 1.

'She doesn't cry with me anymore.' In response to Eli's report that Olivia cried at the end of their school lunch visit. Melissa's comparison inverts the emotional significance — framing Olivia's distress at leaving Eli as evidence of something problematic rather than strong attachment. Delegitimizing comparison (RF-04). Sev 1.

arc_state_snapshot.arc_001_note: ROW-3780 is the weakest ARC-RF02-001 signal to date — a framing statement rather than a blocking event, but documenting the DND's ongoing behavioral influence. No active blocking in this batch.
arc_state_snapshot.arc_002_note: Zero ARC-RF02-002 signals this batch. No preference relays that closed contact. Multiple Melissa-facilitated contacts. Analytically significant as a contrast period — the systematic relay pattern visible in B-007 through B-010 is absent here.
